# Phase 10.0.3 Restoration Continuity Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, runtime restoration systems, deployment restoration systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Define how civilization restores legitimate constitutional continuity if archives, repositories, governance records, or preservation chains become damaged, corrupted, fragmented, or partially lost.

## Scope

- Phase 10 constitutional restoration doctrine.
- Civilization recovery philosophy.
- Governance snapshot restoration doctrine.
- Institutional continuity recovery standards.
- Constitutional archive reconstruction lineage.
- Governance disaster recovery philosophy.
- Preservation restoration verification doctrine.
- Constitutional continuity restoration chains.
- Historical reconstruction authenticity.
- Governance survivability restoration architecture.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No restoration layer creates runtime authority.
- No recovery process bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No restoration chain enables hidden execution escalation.
- No recovery doctrine weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows archive integrity, authenticity, and tamper-evidence doctrine.
- Safe to include now: restoration states, recovery philosophy, snapshot restoration, reconstruction lineage, verification doctrine, restoration chains, authenticity doctrine, and restoration architecture.
- Future upgrade: restoration evidence packet and continuity acceptance checklist.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, runtime restoration systems, deployment restoration systems, or execution systems.
